summary: The paper presents a novel PID-style taxonomy for dynamic weight decay that is conceptually sound, but suffers from (1) fabricated/unsupported numerical claims (AIS=0.566), (2) contradicted unification claims (CWD K_d mapping disproven by fitting data), (3) missing proofs for stated theorems, (4) incomplete ImageNet experiments (4/7 methods), (5) a proposed method (UDWDC) that underperforms NoWD, and (6) three contradictory CSI formulations. The honest reporting of negative results is commendable, but the paper cannot claim 'Unified Framework' when 2/5 methods fail fitting, and cannot list Theorem 1 as a contribution without a proof. Data integrity issues must be resolved before any other improvements.

findings.description: FABRICATED DATA: AIS=0.566 claimed in paper (Section 4.3, Abstract references) has no supporting experimental evidence. The actual measured value from experiments is AIS=0.123 (Spearman rho=0.195). The LOO-CV R^2 for AIS is negative, meaning zero out-of-sample predictive power. This is a data integrity violation that, if published, would constitute scientific misconduct.
findings.suggestion: Replace AIS=0.566 with the actual measured value (0.123). Add explicit acknowledgment that AIS has zero out-of-sample predictive power (LOO-CV R^2 < 0). Remove or heavily qualify claims that AIS is a useful diagnostic metric. Consider demoting AIS from a primary contribution to an exploratory analysis.

findings.description: CWD UNIFICATION CLAIM DIRECTLY CONTRADICTED BY OWN DATA: Table 1 claims CWD maps to 'K_d > 0, Derivative only.' The paper's own fitting data (200-epoch, 3-seed) shows K_p=0.0000, K_i=-0.0000, K_d=-0.0000, offset=-5.014e-5, scale=0.5000. The 4.71% fitting error is achieved ENTIRELY via scale=0.5 (halving the WD magnitude), NOT via K_d. CWD is not derivative control; it is a magnitude halving operation. The unification table is wrong for CWD.
findings.suggestion: Revise Table 1 to accurately reflect that CWD's control-theoretic analog is 'scale reduction (factor 0.5)' rather than 'derivative/alignment feedback (K_d > 0).' Discuss the implication: CWD's accuracy effects may be entirely attributable to WD magnitude reduction, not alignment information. This strengthens the case for the halved-lambda ablation.

findings.description: THEOREM WITHOUT PROOF: Theorem 1 (contraction-rate separation for stagewise SGDW) and Propositions 2-3 are stated without formal proofs or proof sketches. The paper lists 'Theoretical analysis' as Contribution 4. For a NeurIPS submission, missing proofs for stated theorems will trigger desk rejection from theory-oriented reviewers. Theorem 1 claims 'strictly tighter generalization bounds' -- a strong quantitative claim requiring rigorous derivation.
findings.suggestion: Fastest path: downgrade Theorem 1 to 'Conjecture 1' or 'Hypothesis 1', Propositions to 'Observations'. Remove 'Theoretical analysis' from contribution list. This requires 30 minutes of text editing and eliminates a guaranteed reviewer rejection trigger. Alternative: write formal proofs (estimated 2-4 weeks of focused theoretical work).

findings.description: INCOMPLETE IMAGENET EXPERIMENTS: Only 4/7 methods have ImageNet results (CPR, FixedWD, CWD, UDWDC). Missing: SWD, DefazioCorrective, NoWD (3 seeds each = 9 runs). CWD has only 1 seed (no confidence interval computable). Without NoWD baseline, BEM cannot be computed for ImageNet. This has been flagged for 10+ iterations without resolution. The paper acknowledges this gap but still claims 'Comprehensive experiments' as Contribution 5.
findings.suggestion: Complete remaining ImageNet runs: NoWD (3 seeds, critical for BEM), SWD (3 seeds), DefazioCorrective (3 seeds), CWD (2 additional seeds). With 8 GPUs available, this is approximately 2-3 days of compute. Until completion, remove 'Comprehensive' from Contribution 5 and replace with 'Controlled comparison on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and partial ImageNet coverage.'

findings.description: THREE CONTRADICTORY CSI FORMULAS: The paper presents CSI differently in Section 4.2, Table 6 caption, and the text discussion. CSI_temporal = 1/Var_t[lambda_eff] (Section 4.2) vs CSI = 1/(1 + Var_t[lambda_eff^l / mean_t[lambda_eff^l]]) (Table 6 caption). A CSI value of -5.75 (UDWDC temporal) is mathematically impossible under any 1/Var or 1/(1+Var) formulation, as both always produce non-negative values. The reported value reveals an undisclosed normalization or formula.
findings.suggestion: Standardize to one unambiguous formula. Recompute all CSI values from 200-epoch full data (not 10-epoch pilot). Document the exact computation in a reproducibility appendix. If CSI can be negative, the formula must explicitly allow it (e.g., via signed normalization against FixedWD baseline).

findings.description: CPR BUDGET CONFOUND UNRESOLVED: CPR's WD budget is 9.3x FixedWD on CIFAR-10 and 2.3x on ImageNet. The +3.02pp ImageNet advantage cannot be attributed to 'better WD allocation' vs 'simply more regularization' without a budget-matched control (e.g., FixedWD @ lambda=2e-4 or 3e-4). This is the most important unresolved experimental question for the paper's core narrative.
findings.suggestion: Run FixedWD at lambda=2e-4 and lambda=3e-4 on ImageNet (3 seeds each, 90 epochs). If budget-matched FixedWD approaches CPR's accuracy, the narrative shifts to 'integral control merely applies more WD'; if it does not, CPR's feedback mechanism is validated. This experiment costs approximately 4 GPU-hours and resolves the most critical open question.

findings.description: CWD HALVED-LAMBDA ABLATION NEVER EXECUTED: The paper identifies a 50% WD magnitude reduction as a confound (Section 6.3) but has not run the definitive ablation: FixedWD @ lambda=5e-5 vs CWD @ lambda=1e-4. This has been flagged as P0 priority for 3+ iterations. Without this ablation, the paper cannot determine whether CWD's alignment mask contributes anything beyond magnitude reduction -- a question the paper itself raises but does not answer.
findings.suggestion: Run FixedWD @ lambda=5e-5 on CIFAR-10 (3 seeds, 200 epochs) and ImageNet (3 seeds, 90 epochs). Total cost: approximately 7 GPU-hours. If halved-lambda FixedWD matches or beats CWD, the CWD 'alignment feedback' narrative collapses entirely. If CWD still differs, the alignment mask has an effect beyond magnitude. Either outcome strengthens the paper.

findings.description: UDWDC-v2 BN LAYER BUG PRODUCES ABSURD WD BUDGET: UDWDC-v2's WD budget (98599) is 205,000x FixedWD's (0.48) because floor clipping applies lambda_min to all 65 layers including 41 BN layers at every step. BN layers are scale-invariant, so WD on them is theoretically meaningless (just changes effective LR). This is a known bug, not a feature. Reporting v2 results with this bug makes the BEM comparison meaningless.
findings.suggestion: Fix the UDWDC-v2 implementation to exclude BN layers from the control loop (one-line code change). Re-run UDWDC-v2 experiments on CIFAR-10 (3 seeds) and CIFAR-100 (3 seeds). Report corrected WD budget and BEM. This is a software bug, not a theoretical limitation.

findings.description: TITLE OVERPROMISES: 'Unified Feedback Control Framework' implies comprehensive unification, but 2/5 methods (SWD at 45.81%, DefazioCorrective at 37.56%) fail the fitting threshold. The framework unifies alignment-based and constraint-based families only. Scheduling-based methods are explicitly excluded. The title creates a promise the paper does not deliver.
findings.suggestion: Change title to reflect actual scope, e.g., 'A PID-Style Taxonomy for Dynamic Weight Decay: Gains, Instabilities, and Diagnostic Metrics' or 'Gradient-to-Weight Ratio Control: A Diagnostic Framework for Comparing Dynamic Weight Decay Methods.' The current title invites immediate reviewer skepticism.

findings.description: LYAPUNOV FUNCTION INCREASES DURING TRAINING: The Lyapunov function V_t used in Theorem 1's stability analysis empirically increases throughout training, directly contradicting the theorem's guarantee V_{t+1} <= V_t. The paper hand-waves this as 'dominated by mu_t*||w_t||^2 term.' This is the most fundamental theory-experiment contradiction: if V_t increases, the stability certificate is vacuous for observed trajectories.
findings.suggestion: If Theorem 1 is downgraded to Conjecture, this contradiction becomes an open question rather than a falsification. If Theorem 1 is retained, the conditions under which V_t increases must be analyzed and the theorem's applicability domain must be explicitly restricted. Plotting V_t trajectories would make the issue transparent.

findings.description: ALIGNMENT CORRELATION CLAIMS STATISTICALLY IMPROPER: Section 5.7 draws directional conclusions from two non-significant correlations (rho=-0.379, p=0.121; rho=0.045, p=0.858). The claim 'cumulative alignment may be more informative' is unsupported without a formal test (e.g., Steiger's z-test for comparing dependent correlations). Drawing conclusions from p>0.1 correlations is below the statistical standard the paper sets elsewhere (Bonferroni-corrected t-tests).
findings.suggestion: Either (a) run a formal Steiger's z-test comparing the two correlations and report the result, or (b) remove the directional claim entirely and state 'neither correlation is significant at alpha=0.05; no conclusion about relative informativeness is warranted.' Option (b) is safer and faster.

findings.description: BEM NEGATIVE RESULT OMITTED: Under 15-trial Bayesian optimization, the BEM test result is: SWD 68.57% > CWD 68.39% > EqWD 68.30% > FixedWD 68.21%. EqWD does NOT beat tuned FixedWD. This is a conducted negative result that is not reported in the paper. Omitting a completed negative result is more damaging to credibility than disclosing it, especially in a paper that emphasizes honest negative reporting.
findings.suggestion: Add the BEM Bayesian optimization result to Section 5 or 6, with full disclosure that EqWD does not outperform tuned FixedWD. Frame it as: 'BEM identifies efficiency differences but does not predict accuracy ranking, confirming that BEM and accuracy measure orthogonal properties.' This is consistent with the paper's stated philosophy.

findings.description: UDWDC LISTED AS A CONTRIBUTION BUT UNDERPERFORMS NoWD: Contribution 2 presents UDWDC as 'a simple proportional controller.' But UDWDC v1 scores below NoWD (90.15% vs 90.25% on CIFAR-10) and below FixedWD on all benchmarks. Listing a method that harms performance as a 'contribution' is overclaiming. The lessons learned document explicitly flags: 'UDWDC is a diagnostic probe, NOT a contribution.'
findings.suggestion: Reframe Contribution 2: 'We implement UDWDC as a diagnostic probe demonstrating that proportional-only control of rho_t^l is insufficient, providing empirical evidence that integral terms (K_i > 0) are necessary for stable WD feedback control.' This converts a weakness into a finding.

findings.description: FIGURE 1 IS A TIKZ SCHEMATIC, NOT ACTUAL DATA: Figure 1 uses hand-drawn TikZ curves to illustrate rho_t^l trajectories. The caption claims these are from '10-epoch pilot, seed 42.' A schematic drawing masquerading as experimental data is a serious presentation issue. Actual experimental trajectory plots exist in latex/figures/ratio_trajectories.png.
findings.suggestion: Replace the TikZ schematic with the actual experimental plot (ratio_trajectories.png or equivalent from exp/results/). If the actual data does not match the schematic's narrative, the narrative must be revised to match the data.

findings.description: POPULATION vs SAMPLE STD: Tables report +/- values but the ImageNet results (N=3-5) may use population std (ddof=0) rather than sample std (ddof=1). At N=3, the difference is significant: population std underestimates uncertainty by factor sqrt(N/(N-1)) = 1.22. This affects statistical test results and confidence intervals.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all reported uncertainty intervals to sample standard deviation (ddof=1). Add a note in Section 5.1 stating the convention. Recompute all Welch's t-tests using sample standard deviations.

findings.description: NO VIT/TRANSFORMER EXPERIMENTS: The paper discusses Proposition 2 (geometry-corrected alignment for Adam) and mentions ViT several times in related work, but all experiments use CNN architectures with SGD. The framework's applicability to Adam-family optimizers and transformers is entirely hypothetical.
findings.suggestion: Either run a small ViT experiment (ViT-S/16 on CIFAR-100, 3 seeds) to validate generalization, or explicitly scope all claims to 'SGD + CNNs with BN' and move Proposition 2 to a 'Predictions for Future Work' subsection.

findings.description: NO AUGMENTATION INTERACTION ANALYSIS: The paper uses minimal augmentation (RandomCrop + RandomHorizontalFlip) to isolate WD effects, which is a sound design choice. However, CWD's published gains are with full augmentation and higher lambda_base. The paper does not discuss how WD method rankings might change under standard augmentation regimes, which limits practical applicability.
findings.suggestion: Add a brief paragraph in Section 6 discussing the augmentation interaction as a limitation. Optionally, run one or two CIFAR-10 experiments with CutMix/MixUp to test sensitivity.

findings.description: NOTATION INCONSISTENCY: Section 3.4 introduces delta_t as an alias for alpha_t ('delta_t = alpha_t, see notation table') to match Sun et al.'s convention, but no notation table exists in the paper. The dual notation adds confusion without benefit.
findings.suggestion: Either add a notation table to Section 1 or Appendix, or use alpha_t uniformly throughout the paper and note the correspondence to Sun et al.'s delta_t in a footnote.

metric_gaming_risks: UDWDC-v2's 90.36% accuracy is achieved with 205,000x the WD budget of FixedWD. BEM becomes meaningless when the denominator is this extreme. The floor-clipping 'fix' is gaming the WD budget metric by applying WD to scale-invariant BN layers.

CWD's 4.71% fitting error is achieved entirely by scale=0.5 (magnitude halving), not by K_d alignment feedback. The 'unification' success for CWD is a proxy metric that does not validate the claimed control-theoretic interpretation.
AIS=0.566 was a fabricated proxy metric that overstated alignment informativeness by 4.6x vs the actual measurement (0.123). Even the real value lacks out-of-sample predictive power (LOO-CV R^2 < 0).

The paper reports CPR's 3.02pp advantage over FixedWD on ImageNet without budget-matched controls. CPR uses 2.3x more WD budget. The accuracy improvement may be entirely due to stronger regularization, not better allocation.
novelty_risks: The PID taxonomy of WD methods is descriptive rather than prescriptive: it shows existing methods can be mapped to gain configurations but does not prove this mapping is optimal or generative (it cannot design better methods).

PIDAO (Nature Communications 2024) already applied PID control to optimization. While the control targets differ (gradient update vs WD coefficient), reviewers may see this as incremental extension of PID-in-optimization.

BEM, CSI, and AIS are the main novel metric contributions, but AIS lacks predictive power (LOO-CV R^2 < 0), CSI has three contradictory formulas, and BEM cannot be computed on ImageNet (missing NoWD baseline). All three metrics need validation work.
CWD's alignment-aware contribution may reduce to simple WD magnitude halving, which would invalidate one of the four 'sub-traditions' the paper claims to unify.
reproducibility_gaps: No raw experimental data files present in exp/results/ -- all result directories are empty. The paper's numbers cannot be independently verified from the workspace artifacts.
CSI formula is ambiguous across three locations in the paper; different formulas produce different results.
UDWDC-v2 implementation contains a known BN-layer bug that inflates WD budget by 205,000x. Reproducing reported v2 numbers requires this specific bug.
ImageNet experiments are incomplete (4/7 methods, CWD single-seed); no other group could replicate the 'full comparison' because it does not exist.
Population vs sample std convention undocumented; affects reproducibility of statistical tests.
Theorem 1 and Propositions 2-3 have no proofs; cannot be independently verified.
Figure 1 uses hand-drawn TikZ rather than actual data; the visual evidence is not reproducible from experimental outputs.
